codeReview.summary: The Phase 5 checker covers most listed behaviors, but trait impl signature checking ignores receiver (`self`) shape entirely. As a result, impls can satisfy trait contracts even when the mutability/borrowing mode of `self` is incompatible with the trait declaration.

issues.description: Trait method signatures are recorded using only explicit parameter types and return type; `sig_self` / `fn_self` is discarded. Later impl validation compares only parameter count/types and return type, so a trait requiring `fn touch(&mut self)` is incorrectly satisfied by `impl Trait for T { fn touch(&self) { ... } }` (and vice versa). This violates the trait contract and will allow non-mutating receivers to pass typecheck where mutation capability is required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill instructs reading `.factory/services.yaml`, but this repo stores services metadata at `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/services.yaml` and the mission instructions referenced a non-existent `/home/lenstr/.factory/services.yaml`. Shared state should clarify the repo-root-relative location to avoid failed lookups.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Attempting to read `/home/lenstr/.factory/services.yaml` returned ENOENT during review, while the repo clearly contains `.factory/library/*` under `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/`.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, skill file, architecture/environment docs, and commit `04b6154`. Found one high-confidence blocking correctness issue: trait impl validation does not compare `self` receiver kind, so incompatible `&self`/`&mut self` trait method implementations are accepted.
